mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.5 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.5%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during late-cycle expansion indicates capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes margin compression and earnings revisions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of observations, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently correlates with negative 20-day equity returns, or the rolling Spearman correlation between GDP growth and SPX forward returns remains below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 2.5 percent annualized growth, it triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for more than 65% of the time following CPI spikes above 2.5%, or forward returns show no statistically significant decline relative to low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and increases in nonfarm payrolls confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which accelerates wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving credit balances indicate expanding consumer leverage and spending capacity, which boosts retail and service sector earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during credit expansion phases.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations when RSAFS rises, or the signal's predictive power disappears during periods of high credit spreads.
